_comment: Example config. Copy to PatchManager.config.json and edit locally; do not commit personal runtime config.
_comment_scope: ScopeProfile: Personal (full coverage, single machine), Commercial (full coverage + fleet behaviours - the safe default for organisations), or CommercialManaged (Intune/SCCM/RMM already owns OS/Office/browsers; PatchManager descopes those and covers the third-party gap).

MaintenanceWindow._comment_jitter: JitterMaxMinutes: null = decide by profile (Personal 0, Commercial 120). Jitter staggers fleet-wide runs; a single personal device does not need it.

Network._comment: BITSThrottleEnabled: null = decide by profile (Personal off, Commercial on). Throttle applies for the run only and is reverted afterwards.

PackageManagers._comment_choco: ChocolateyEnabled: null = decide by profile (Personal on - the CLI is free; Commercial/CommercialManaged off). The choco CLI is free, but Chocolatey for Business is a paid product - enabling it in a commercial context is your explicit licence decision. Set true/false to override the profile default.
PackageManagers._comment_scoop: Scoop is per-user and only runs in a user-context session (never SYSTEM). Off under CommercialManaged.

VendorUpdaters._comment: Drives silent 'apply update now' updaters for apps not covered by an actionable WinGet candidate (built-in: Brave). Off under CommercialManaged. Add your own Omaha-style updaters via ExtraCatalogue, each with: Name, PackageId, Provider, WinGetOverlapId, VersionRegistryPaths, VersionValueName, UpdaterPathCandidates, UpdaterArgs.

StalenessReport._comment: Report-only exposure checks - never patches. Findings appear in their own report section and are excluded from applied/failed counts. On for all profiles (a safety net, like the inventory KEV scan). Covers Microsoft Defender signature age, Windows feature-update lag, and installed developer runtime versions.

Firmware._comment: OEM firmware/BIOS/driver updates via Dell Command Update / HP Image Assistant / Lenovo System Update. OFF by default for every profile - firmware can require AC power and reboots and carries real risk. Enable deliberately. Skipped on battery; never reboots on its own.

Notifications._comment: Optional webhook run summary. Works with Teams/Slack incoming webhooks and generic JSON receivers. OnlyOnProblems limits posts to runs with failures, KEV matches, SLA breaches, or errors.

SelfUpdate._comment: Keep PatchManager itself current from GitHub. Enabled: null = decide by profile (Personal and Commercial on, CommercialManaged off - a managed estate's platform should own how PatchManager is deployed). Ref 'latest' tracks the newest PUBLISHED release tag only (never a moving branch, never a pre-release); pin a specific tag, or use 'main' to track the branch. Downloads are version-gated and parse-validated; set ExpectedSha256 to lock an exact build. Git-clone installs are supported: self-update replaces only Invoke-PatchManager.ps1; use git pull when you want the full repo refreshed. AutoApply=false reports updates without changing the script. A new script is never executed in the run that fetched it.
